title: Schauwerk Miro managed image lifecycle v1
goal: Close the remaining managed-image lifecycle gap with a separately authorized Miro REST authority for bounded image read/delete operations, without reusing MCP OAuth, widening the existing Companion app scopes, enabling paid features or weakening fail-closed mutation rules.
completion: A separately authorized Miro REST credential path is configured outside the repository and passes the Schauwerk write-authority doctor without reusing MCP OAuth.
One isolated create-verify-delete image saga is proven against a disposable test image with exact board/item binding and digest-bound receipts.
Managed image lifecycle availability becomes true only after live authorization is proven; missing or insufficient REST authority remains fail-closed.
The existing Schauwerk Companion app identity, URL and scopes remain unchanged, no credentials enter Git or receipts, and no paid provider feature is enabled.

metadata.source_observation: The live Miro MCP exposes image create/read helpers but no image_delete. Schauwerk already implements a separate REST read/delete adapter and reports the cross-surface managed-image lane unavailable because no separate REST credential is installed and live write authorization is therefore unproven.
does_not_establish: permission to reuse MCP OAuth for REST
permission to widen the existing Companion Developer App scopes
permission to enable paid provider features
